name: Bordeaux Finish Area
roadSegment: Official Bordeaux finish coordinate from app data; exact finish-line street/perimeter needs verification against final map
summary: Maximum atmosphere and ceremony option for the Bordeaux sprint finish, but with the highest urban crowd and perimeter uncertainty.
whyItMatters: The finish gives podium, announcers, and sprint-stage spectacle; the viewing guide warns that finish barriers require very early arrival and have low flexibility.

access.estimated_route_from_base: Approach by walking/transit from Bordeaux base before the finish perimeter fills; exact lodging not assumed.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Urban transit/walking preferred. Parking near the finish is unknown and likely restricted.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Plan to stay on one side of the course once barriers are active; do not assume course crossing after the caravan.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown; official Bordeaux/Gironde finish-area security and traffic plans required.

pros: Full finish-line Tour atmosphere: sprint, announcers, podium, media, and sponsor activity.
Official Tour schedule gives Bordeaux finish timing.
Easy to combine with Bordeaux lodging/food after the race if exits are planned.
cons: Likely densest Stage 7 crowd and most controlled public space.
Good barrier views may require arriving many hours early.
Final-kilometer areas may be VIP/media/team/technical rather than public.

logistics.parking: Unknown; assume no close parking until official plan says otherwise.
logistics.transit: Bordeaux tram/bus access likely useful in normal conditions, but event-day reroutes and station crowd controls must be verified.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official finish map, fan-zone map, and traffic order.

crowdRisk: very high: flat-stage city finish in a major city.
bathroomFoodProximity: Potentially good in central Bordeaux, but finish barriers may make facilities unreachable; official fan-zone/toilet map needed.
escapeRoute: After the finish, walk away from the finish compound toward pre-selected transit/open streets. Do not depend on crossing the course immediately after the sprint.
safetyAssessment: Use only official spectator barriers. Sprint finishes involve high speed plus race vehicles; avoid leaning, signs, phones, or bags over the barrier.

timing.best_arrival_window: 4-6+ hours before finish; earlier if the group wants front barrier or podium proximity.
timing.race_day_risk: High until exact finish-line, fan-zone, and security-perimeter details are published.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 7 official page
sources.note: Supports Stage 7 route, flat finish type, official Bordeaux finish timing, and final approach via Pont Simone Veil/Bordeaux entry.

sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 7 route geometry; candidate coordinate comes from current app data and should be checked against final ASO map.

sources.note: Supports general tram/bus network context in Bordeaux; event-day reroutes are unknown.

open_questions: What is the exact Bordeaux finish-line street and final-kilometer public perimeter?
Where are official public fan zones, screens, toilets, and water points?
